title: alnus alnobetula subsp. fruticosa
intro: Alnus alnobetula subsp. fruticosa, also known as mountain alder, is a subspecies of the green alder tree, characterized by its shrub-like growth habit and ability to thrive in cold, dry environments. This plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Eastern Europe and Russia, for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Alnus alnobetula subsp. fruticosa include the reduction of inflammation and the alleviation of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to act as an antiseptic and antioxidant. Herbal preparations of Alnus alnobetula subsp. fruticosa include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
